kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: At least one of Edmund and the Blacksmith belongs to the South Side.
The Weaver's levy is exactly 3 coins more than Edmund.
If Cooper goes with Wulfric, then Tanner goes with Southgate.
Edmund, Rowan, and Wulfric go with Carpenter, Southgate, and 6 coins, in some order.
Either Blacksmith goes with Matilda or someone in the Gate Ward goes with Matilda (but not both).
Beatrix, Edmund, and 6 coins go with Tanner, Millpond, and Riverside, in some order.
Either Carpenter goes with Bridgegate or someone in the Gate Ward goes with Beatrix.
More members of the North Side are in the Clothiers' Guild than members of the South Side.
Carpenter goes with exactly two of Rowan, Riverside, and 10 coins.
Exactly one member of the Ironmongers' Guild is in the Hill Ward.
The Potter's levy is higher than both Edmund and the artisan in Highrow.
Exactly two of Cedric, the Carpenter, and the artisan with 9 coins belong to the North Side.

chain: Start by assuming Beatrix goes with Carpenter.

So Beatrix does not go with Carpenter.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: At least one of Gus and the angler who landed the Bass belongs to the East Watershed.
The angler who landed the Pike's weight is exactly 3 lb more than Gus.
If Carp goes with Wade, then Perch goes with Stillwater.
Gus, Royce, and Wade go with Bluegill, Stillwater, and 11 lb, in some order.
Either Bass goes with Mabel or someone in the South Watershed goes with Mabel (but not both).
Boone, Gus, and 11 lb go with Perch, Grayreach, and Pinehollow, in some order.
Either Bluegill goes with Birchpool or someone in the South Watershed goes with Boone.
More members of the Lowland Basin are in the Pike Family than members of the East Watershed.
Bluegill goes with exactly two of Royce, Pinehollow, and 15 lb.
Exactly one member of the Sunfish Family is in the North Watershed.
The angler who landed the Crappie's weight is higher than both Gus and the angler at Foxglen.
Exactly two of Esther, the angler who landed the Bluegill, and the catch with 14 lb belong to the Lowland Basin.
